text:
REGIME — Defensive rotation unwound, tech and energy bid, metals lagged.

INDICES — VIX 24.2, down on the day. QQQ +0.60%, IWM +0.43%, SPY +0.47%, DIA +0.37%. Small caps outperformed large caps on the day. VIXY fell -2.45%, the sharpest cross-asset move, signaling fear compression.

SECTORS — XLP +0.94%, XLY +0.82%, XLE +0.73%, XLF +0.71%, XLK +0.58%, XLI +0.51%, XLRE +0.36%, XLC +0.05%, XLV -0.36%, XLU -0.37%, XLB -0.38%. Top industries: MSOS +6.23% and IBIT +4.08%; bottom industries: TAN -1.26% and LIT -1.42%.

THE 7s — Defense +1.25%, Fab +1.22%, Housing +1.08%, Toll Road +1.00%, Semi +0.84%, Bank +0.80%, Industrial +0.66%, Energy +0.57%, New Staples +0.51%, Old Staples +0.45%, Insurance +0.44%, Freight +0.35%, Cloud +0.19%, Old Metals +0.05%, Medtech -0.16%, Power -0.27%, Health -0.39%, Buildout -0.40%, Software -0.63%, New Metals -0.85%. Strongest ticker in Defense 7 not specified; weakest in New Metals 7 not specified in data.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.4, Neutral. Top movers: MSOS +6.23% (Industries), IBIT +4.08% (Industries), FC +44.00% (Consumer Defensive). 20d momentum best sector: XLE +6.19%; worst: XLC -4.55%. Best basket 20d: Energy +8.57%; worst: Software -10.72%. Tech's +0.58% gain in XLK was driven by Iran de-escalation hopes easing macro risk appetite.

WATCH — EATZ today +2.02%, volume 10.5x average, momentum breakout on unusual participation warrants confirmation. IJK +0.28% on 8.0x volume with muted price reaction suggests positioning shift in mid-cap growth worth monitoring. SLV +0.46% today but -12.97% over 20 days with GLD -0.41%; precious metals divergence against rising USO +0.74% flags a cross-asset dislocation.
